summary.intent: Fix the Gossamer Electron app's loading screen bug, resolve git polling issues with the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch, and improve checkpoint display in the SessionDetail UI with better formatting, markdown support, and a proper diff viewer.
summary.outcome: Fixed the loading screen by making git fetch async, restored the start script with concurrently, fixed the poll loop to use refs/heads/ for local branch resolution, added a git diff endpoint, and built a custom diff2html-based diff viewer with full light/dark mode support matching the app's Mantine theme.

repo: Gossamer uses a git worktree at .gossamer/checkpoints checked out from entire/checkpoints/v1; the indexer polls this worktree and resets it to the local branch tip on each cycle.

The entire CLI post-commit hook (entire hooks git post-commit) creates checkpoint commits on entire/checkpoints/v1 and pushes to remote; if push fails, it reverts the local branch with git reset --hard origin/entire/checkpoints/v1.
Shadow/in-progress sessions live on branches like entire/<hash>-<hash> and only appear in checkpoints after the post-commit hook finalizes them.
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin uses gitOid as @id, not checkpointId — must use findFirst({ where: { checkpointId } }) not findUnique.
The app uses light-dark(lightVal, darkVal) CSS function tied to Mantine's color-scheme attribute, NOT @media prefers-color-scheme — all theme-aware CSS must use this pattern.

diff2html colorScheme: 'auto' uses @media prefers-color-scheme internally, which conflicts with Mantine's light-dark() approach; must use colorScheme: 'light' and override everything with light-dark() in CSS.
InlineMarkdown and MarkdownView components already exist in app/src/renderer/src/components/MarkdownView.tsx and handle fenced code, inline code, bold, italic, tables, and lists.

code.finding: execSync for git fetch blocked the entire Node.js event loop — no HTTP requests could be served while git hung on SSH passphrase. Fixed by using promisified exec with a 10s timeout.

git reset --hard entire/checkpoints/v1 (without refs/heads/ prefix) was resolving to origin/entire/checkpoints/v1 when both refs pointed to the same SHA, causing the poller to pin the worktree to origin rather than local. Fixed with refs/heads/ prefix.

merge --ff-only in runIndex was inadvertently fast-forwarding the local branch to match origin, which caused the local branch to pick up a bad commit pushed during testing. Removed entirely — entire CLI is solely responsible for advancing refs/heads/entire/checkpoints/v1.

diff2html's own CSS variables (--d2h-*) at :root are declared after any override attempt without !important, so CSS variable overrides silently lose. Using var(--mantine-color-*) with light-dark() in direct property rules with !important is the reliable pattern.

Files changed section was hidden when fileCount=0 and diffStats=null (initial state), meaning users never saw the section appear at all on expand. Must show unconditionally when expanded and display 'Loading...' while fetch is in flight.

workflow: When diagnosing silent hook failures, removing 2>/dev/null || true and running the hook manually immediately reveals the real error.
git worktree add --no-track prevents the worktree branch from getting a remote tracking config, which avoids implicit remote resolution.
GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 and BatchMode=yes only suppress terminal prompts; macOS SSH Keychain shows a GUI dialog that bypasses both — not a reliable suppression method.

diff2html renders well in light mode out of the box but needs explicit CSS overrides for every background/color class to work in dark mode; using the library's CSS custom properties approach only works if you can guarantee override order.

friction: Multiple rounds of SSH suppression attempts (GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT, BatchMode=yes, SSH_AUTH_SOCK clearing, --no-track) all failed before user decided to revert and rely on user's SSH agent being configured correctly.
Accidental destructive git commit during push testing: git commit --allow-empty staged pending deletions from a previous failed hook run and pushed them to origin, requiring a force-push to recover.
merge --ff-only in runIndex silently advanced the local branch to match the bad origin state after the accidental push, compounding the damage.
diff2html dark mode required multiple CSS iteration rounds — CSS variable approach, then @media approach, then !important approach — before settling on light-dark() with colorScheme: 'light'.
diff2html shows filename twice by default (file list summary + per-file header); had to hide .d2h-file-list with display:none.
The entire post-commit hook was silently failing due to 2>/dev/null || true, making it impossible to diagnose why checkpoints weren't appearing.
open_items: filesTouched is empty in CheckpointSessionMetadata and CheckpointMetadata for checkpoint d2014fd45299 — the indexer may not be reading files_touched from metadata.json correctly, or the field is absent in that checkpoint's metadata.
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin mapping may not exist for some checkpoints (e.g. d2014fd45299), causing the diff endpoint to return [] — no git OID means no diff can be shown.

Checkpoint 18f992950c10 referenced in commit 35f7367 was never written to entire/checkpoints/v1 (hook misfired silently) — that checkpoint data is permanently lost with no way to retroactively recreate it.
The entire post-commit hook has 2>/dev/null || true suppressing all errors — should be made more observable so silent failures are caught earlier.
npm run app script was added as a convenience alias to cd app && npm run dev but is arguably redundant now that npm start launches both; consider whether to keep it.
